violations.text: A knife was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026

At the time of inspection, ROP tuna was thawing with package intact. Reduced oxygen packaging fish that bears a label indicating that it is to be kept frozen until time of use shall be removed from the reduced oxygen environment. Temperature was observed to be at 30 degrees Fahrenheit; PIC opened package at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/26/2026
Walk-in cooler was not in good repair; inspector observed an active leak dripping into a container. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2026

Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was 25 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC took unit out of service. Establishment to sanitize in 3 bay sink until serviced and dispensing proper concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

Sauces were observed held out of temperature control at 52 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per interview with PIC, sauce has been out for less than 1 hour, sauces were moved to prep unit at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The door to the outside, located in the kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

A knife was stored on an unclean surface (behind hand sink wall) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
Ceilings tiles in the kitchen have stains from previous water damage. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026
The condensate from the beer cooler refrigeration unit is draining into a container. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/26/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2026

The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. Per PIC, new menus have been ordered with asterisks indicating which foods are raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Dishes and utensils were observed in the hand-washing sink at the cookline. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023
Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. Last date of sale were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) was observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service at the time of the inspection and ice was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Pickled peppers and pico de gallo prepared on-site were observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. PIC voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Sauces were placed hot in the walk-in refrigerator at 160 degrees Fahrenheit in containers greater than four inches deep, covered, and not fully submerged in an ice bath. PIC removed items from the walk-in refrigerator and into a new ice bath to properly cool items before placing them in the walk-in refrigerator. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged tuna was observed thawing with the package intact at 37 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC opened the packages of tuna at the time of inspection to properly thaw. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients (nuts, sesame seeds, etc.) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 12/15/2023
